mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off positioning among institutional investors, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in residential housing starts and a decline in consumer credit outstanding signals a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50 percent of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a sustained rise in industrial production signals supply-side capacity expansion that outpaces demand, reducing input cost pressures and boosting corporate margins, which yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when industrial output rises alongside inflation, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52 percent over any 3-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical median trend rate reflects robust aggregate demand that directly supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative or zero rank correlation with forward returns across all quarterly observation windows, or earnings revision momentum contradicts the GDP growth signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital becomes structurally elevated, compressing equity risk premiums and forcing a re-rating of growth-oriented equities, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite rates remaining above the 4.5 percent threshold, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between high-rate and low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate simultaneously increases indicates weak labor quality and rising underemployment, signaling impending wage-price rigidity that dampens profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55 percent of monthly observation windows, or the joint series exhibit no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, a sharp month-over-month increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader consumer spending acceleration, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Housing starts rise but equity returns remain flat or negative, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 50 percent when tested against out-of-sample monthly data.
